title: Amauropelta rosulata
taxonomy: Amauropelta rosulata is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, a family of ferns. This plant belongs to the genus Amauropelta, which consists of species of terrestrial ferns. Amauropelta rosulata is native to South America, specifically in the tropical regions of the continent. It is a type of deciduous fern, meaning it loses its leaves seasonally. The classification of Amauropelta rosulata is further supported by its unique combination of leaf morphology and chromosome count.

intro: Amauropelta rosulata, also known as the Rosulate Amauropelta, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against cellular damage. The bioactive constituents of Amauropelta rosulata include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
